translated: If you do not do good today, you will not receive a good reward tomorrow.

ref: It returned in the evening, holding in its beak a green leaf from an olive tree. Noah knew the water was finally going down.
text: Ni okpo le̱ ba eṅõ gbe̱ke̱nãši, ni na, mũtšo-bāfo̱ kõ enā; ni Noa ná le ake̱ nui le̱ emī̃ ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.
translated: When people began to increase in number on the earth, and daughters were born to them; Noah was a righteous man, blameless among the people of his time.

ref: I promise every living creature that the earth and those living on it will never again be destroyed by a flood.
text: Ni mahã mikpãṅmo̱ ní mike̱nye ekpãṅ le̱ amã ši, ni nu afua le̱ ebaplenẽṅ helo fẽ doṅṅ, ni asaṅ nu afua ko ebakpãtãṅ šikpoṅ le̱ hĩe gbĩkogbĩko.
translated: If you do not take good care of your body, it will become weak and sick, and you will not be able to do your daily activities well.

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.
text: Miṅõ mikwãbue̱te̱ mito atatui le̱ amli, ni efe kpāṅmo̱ ni yo̱ mike̱ šikpoṅ le̱ teṅ le̱ he okadi.
translated: If you do not listen to what I am saying, you will not understand and you will make mistakes in the future.

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Ši Šem ke̱ Iafet ko̱ mama ni ame̱ṅõ-fõ ame̱yi enyo̱ le̱ fẽ ame̱kõdši ano̱, ni ame̱nyie se̱se̱, ni ame̱te ni ame̱ke̱-yaha ame̱tše̱ yayaiaṅ; ši ame̱tšṍmo̱ ame̱hĩeaṅ ame̱hã se̱gbe̱, ni ame̱naa ame̱tše̱ yayaiaṅ.
translated: But Shem and Japheth took a garment and laid it on both their shoulders, and went backward and covered the nakedness of their father; their faces were turned away, and they did not see their father's nakedness.

ref: I pray God will give Japheth more and more land and let him take over the territory of Shem. May Canaan be his slave.”
text: Nyoṅmo̱ aabā Iafet mli alē̱, ni *eehī Šem bũi le̱ amli, ni Kanaan aatšṍ ame̱-nyṍṅ! *Ef.
translated: May God enlarge Japheth, and let him dwell in the tents of Shem, and let Canaan be his servant! Gen.

ref: Japheth's descendants had their own languages, tribes, and land. They were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras. Gomer was the ancestor of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah. Javan was the ancestor of Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim, who settled along the coast. The Descendants of Ham
text: Mene̱mei amli mei dše̱ ſã dšeṅmādši le̱ aṅšo̱kpo̱i le̱ ano̱, ye̱ ame̱šikpõdši le̱ ano̱, mofẽmo ke̱ ewiemo̱, ye̱ ame̱kutšei ke̱ ame̱dšeṅmādši le̱ anā. Zak.
translated: Some people do not know what they are supposed to do, and so, they follow others, but it is good to know yourself, and do what you are meant to do. End.

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Mene̱mei dši Noa bii le̱ awekui ye̱ ame̱sešibii le̱ anã ye̱ ame̱mãdši le̱ amli; ni mene̱mei amli dšeṅmãdši le̱ dše̱ fã šikpoṅ le̱ ṅo̱ ye̱ nu afua le̱ se̱.
translated: Just as Noah entered the ark with his family and all living creatures, so must we enter into God’s protection with faith and obedience.

text: Ni beni Šem fo̱ Arpakšad se̱ le̱, eye afii ohai-enumo̱, ni efo̱ bihĩĩ ke̱ biyei.

translated: If you are patient, work hard, and do not give up, then whatever you desire will come to you, and your efforts will not be in vain.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.

translated: Abram was seventy-five years old when he left Haran, and he took his wife, his nephew, all their possessions, and the people they had acquired in Haran.

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Mẽba okē̱ aké̱: Minyemiyō dšile? nto kũlē̱ mibaṅõlē̱ mafe miṅã. Ni agbe̱né̱, na oṅã, ṅõle̱ ni oke̱le̱ aya!
translated: My child said: Why are you sad? Don’t worry, everything will be fine. If you believe, then yes, it will surely be well with you!

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Abram fā ebū le̱ ni eba ebahī Mamre tšei kutū ni yo̱ Hebron le̱ ašiši, ni emā afo̱lešàlate̱ ye̱ dše̱i ehã Iehowa.
translated: Abram moved his tent and went to live near the great trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar to the Lord.

ref: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “All I want are my people. You can keep everything else.”
text: Ni Sodom maṅtše̱ le̱ kē̱ Abram ake̱: Hãmi gbomei le̱, ni oṅõ nii le̱ ohã ohe.
translated: Then the king of Sodom said to Abram: Give me the people, and take the goods for yourself.

ref: Abram answered: The Lord God Most High made the heavens and the earth. And I have promised him
text: Ni Abram kē̱ Sodom maṅtše̱ le̱ ake̱: Mīwó minine no̱ mītšõ Iehowa ṅwei flōflo Nyoṅmo̱ ni dši ṅwei ke̱ šikpoṅ tše̱ le̱,
translated: Abram replied to Sodom and said: I have raised my hand to the Lord, God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth,

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Ni lofõdši lōyelo̱i kpleke ši basõmo̱ gbohĩĩ le̱ ano̱, ni Abram ſeame̱.

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Hagar, Sarai weyō, nẽgbe̱ odše̱, ni nẽgbe̱ hũ oyaä? Ni ekē̱: Miwura Sarai hĩe midšoo̱ foi ye̱ ne̱.
translated: And he said to her: Hagar, Sarai’s maid, where have you come from, and where are you going? And she said: I am fleeing from Sarai my mistress.

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Ni etšē̱ Iehowa ni ke̱le̱ wie le̱ gbe̱i ake̱: Bo dši Mawu ni naämi! Edšake̱ ekē̱: Ani bie̱ ne̱ hũ mikwe̱ moni naämi le̱ yi se̱ lo?
translated: This is what Jehovah has said: Say, "God has remembered!" Then ask: Can a woman forget her nursing child?

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Ni mike̱ mikpãṅmo̱ le̱ aṅmẽ mike̱o teṅ, ni mahã oyi afá babaō dientše̱.
translated: If you do not listen to what I am telling you, then you will not know what your father said.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ sā kē̱ Abraham ake̱: Ši bo hũ, ye mikpãṅmo̱ le̱ no̱, bo ke̱ osešibii ni aaba ye̱ ose̱ le̱ ye̱ ame̱yino̱i le̱ amli.
translated: But God said to Abraham: Do not be sad, listen to what she says, do as your wife Sarah tells you, because it is through Isaac that your descendants will be named.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ sã kē̱ Abraham ake̱: Oṅã Sarai le̱, kātšé̱ egbe̱i ake̱ Sarai doṅṅ, ši Sara dši egbe̱i.
translated: And God said to Abraham: Your wife Sarai, you shall not call her name Sarai anymore, but her name shall be Sarah.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱: Le̱le̱ṅ, oṅã Sara aafo̱ binū ahão, ni oowole̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Isak; ni mahã mike̱le̱ kpãṅmo̱ le̱ amã ši, ni atšṍ *nãno̱ kpãṅmo̱ ahã éseši ni aaba ye̱ ese̱ le̱.
translated: And God said: Indeed, Sarah your wife will bear you a son, and you will call his name Isaac; and I will establish my covenant with him, and with his descendants after him as an everlasting covenant.

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: *makpleke ši mate agbe̱ne̱, ni †mayakwe̱ ake̱, bo̱ni blo̱mo̱ le̱ ba miṅõ le̱, ame̱fe nakai kwrā lo; ni ke̱-dšee nakai hũ, ni male. *
translated: Do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself; each day has enough trouble of its own.

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Ni ame̱hã ame̱tše̱ wein enu nakai nyō le̱ṅ; ni onukpa le̱ yiṅ te, ni eke̱ etše̱ yawo̱; ni elee ešikãmo̱ mli lo ešitē mli.
translated: If someone slaps you on one cheek, turn the other to them also; if they take your cloak, do not stop them from taking your tunic; if they ask you for something, give it to them.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ ba Abimelek ṅõ nyōṅ ye̱ lamõ mli, ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Na, obagbo ye̱ yō le̱ ní oṅõ le̱ hewo̱, edšake̱ nū ṅã dšile̱.
translated: Then God came to Abimelech in a dream at night and said to him: Look, you are as good as dead because of the woman you have taken; she is a married woman.

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
text: Ni Abimelek kē̱: Na, mišikpoṅ le̱ kã ohĩe ne̱; hĩ heni osumoo̱.
translated: Then Abimelech said: See, my land is before you; settle wherever you please.

ref: The Lord was good to Sarah and kept his promise.
text: Ni Iehowa basra Sara, take̱ bo̱ni ekē̱ le̱, ni Iehowa fe ehã Sara, take̱ bo̱ni ewie le̱.
translated: When the Lord remembered Sarah, she became pregnant, and when the Lord visited Sarah, she gave birth.

ref: Although Abraham was very old, Sarah had a son exactly at the time God had said.
text: Ni Sara ná mũsũ, ni eke̱ Abraham fo̱ binū ye̱ egbo̱le̱ mli, ye̱ be ni Nyoṅmo̱ ekē̱le̱ le̱ mli. Heb.
translated: When Sarah gave birth, she and Abraham named the child, saying that God had given them joy. Heb.

text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ gble ehĩṅme̱ii, ni ena nubu ko; ni eyiṅ ete, ni eyaye̱ nu ewo woloto̱ le̱ obõ, ni eṅõ-hã oblanyo le̱ enu.
translated: When God speaks to someone, and the person listens; and believes, and does what He commands, then that person is blessed.

ref: About this time Abimelech and his army commander Phicol said to Abraham, “God blesses everything you do!
text: Ni eba mli ye̱ nakai be le̱ mli ake̱ Abimelek ke̱ etatše̱ Fikol bakē̱ Abraham ake̱: Nyoṅmo̱ ke̱o ye̱ ye̱ nibii fẽ ní ofeo̱ le̱ mli.
translated: At that time, Abimelek together with Phicol the commander of his army said to Abraham: God is with you in everything you do.

text: Nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱ kã Nyoṅmo̱ kitã otšõmi ye̱ bie̱ ne̱, ake̱ oyeṅmi lo mibii ke̱ miho̱i akũtũmpo̱o; ši edšũrõ bo̱ni *mifeo le̱, nakai ooṅō-femi ke̱ šikpoṅ ni obato gbo̱ ye̱ no̱ ne̱. *
translated: If you believe that God created the world, then you should also believe that He has the power to protect it; so do not be afraid, but rather trust in His wisdom and the guidance He gives you.

ref: Abraham gave Abimelech some sheep and cattle, then the two men made a peace treaty.

text: Ni ṅme̱ne̱ miba nubu le̱ he, ni mikē̱: Iehowa, minuṅtšo̱ Abraham Nyoṅmo̱! ke̱dši oobã migbe̱ ni minyie ne̱ adša le̱,

translated: So, when they saw that the thing was not good at all in the town, the elders then gathered together.

ref: But you couldn't tell it, because these skinny cows were just as skinny as they were before. At once, I woke up.
text: Ni beni ame̱bote ame̱mũsũṅ le̱, anaa ake̱ ame̱yeame̱; ši moṅ ame̱be ye̱ nyãṅemo̱ lolo tamo̱ tšutšu le̱ noṅṅ. Ke̱ke̱ ni mihĩe tšẽ.
translated: If someone helps you, or does you a favor; you too should help others when you get the chance. That is how life should be.

ref: I also dreamed that I saw seven heads of grain growing on one stalk. The heads were full and ripe.
text: Ni misā mikwe̱ ye̱ milamõ le̱ mli, ni na, abletšei kpawo ba tšo kome yi, eba kpokplō.
translated: If I had known you were coming here today, I would have prepared something for you, but it's too late now.

ref: These heads of grain swallowed the full ones. I told my dreams to the magicians, but none of them could tell me the meaning of the dreams.
text: Ni abletšei legelegei le̱ mĩ abletšei kpakpai kpawo le̱. Ni midšadše ene̱ mitšõ woloṅlelo̱i le̱, ši mokomoko enaa he eko ekē̱ē̱mi.
translated: If you treat children well, the children will also treat you well. If you speak to them with kindness, they will always listen to what you say.

ref: The seven skinny, ugly cows that came up later also stand for seven years, as do the seven bad heads of grain that were scorched by the desert wind. The dreams mean there will be seven years when there won't be enough grain.
text: Ni tšinai kpawo ní etrã lõ, ni he yo̱ nyãṅemo̱, ni nyie ame̱se̱ ba le̱, afii kpawoṅ, ni abletšei kpawo ni noko be̱ yi ni bokã kō̱yo egbĩame̱ keklē le̱, hõmo̱yeli afii kpawo ni.
translated: If someone does something bad to you, and you keep it in your heart, and you meet the person again, and you remember what happened and decide to do the same thing back to them, then you are just like that person.

ref: You are now governor of all Egypt!”
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Na, miṅõo mito Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ fẽ no̱. Bo̱f.
translated: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph: Well, I have put you in charge of all Egypt. Go ahead.

ref: He gave Joseph the Egyptian name Zaphenath Paneah. And he let him marry Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, a priest in the city of Heliopolis. Joseph traveled all over Egypt.
text: Ni Farao wo Iosef gbe̱i ake̱ Zafenat-Panea; ni eṅõ On oso̱fo Potifera biyō Asenat le̱ ehãle̱ gblã. Ni Iosef dše kpo eyakpa Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.
translated: Then Pharaoh gave Joseph the name Zaphenath-Paneah; and he gave him Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, priest of On, as his wife. And Joseph went throughout the land of Egypt.

ref: In fact, there was so much grain that they stopped keeping record, because it was like counting the grains of sand along the beach.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iosef bua able nā eto babaō dientše̱ take̱ ṅšo̱ nā šia, ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni efõ kanemo̱; edšake̱ ebe̱ yikule̱.
translated: Because Joseph was a descendant of David, he went up from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to Bethlehem the town of David; he went there to register.

ref: But Jacob did not send Joseph's younger brother Benjamin with them; he was afraid that something might happen to him.
text: Ši Iosef nyemi Benyamin le̱, Iakob hãã eke̱ enyemimei le̱ aya; edšake̱ ekē̱: Koni aleenõ ošrã ko akayašrãle̱.
translated: When Joseph brought Benjamin, Jacob was very happy to see his children; he said: Now I am truly happy and content.

ref: “No sir,” they replied. “We're your servants, and we have only come to buy grain.
text: Ni ame̱kē̱le̱ ake̱: Dabi, minuṅtšo̱, ši niyenii hemo̱ otšũdši ba.
translated: If someone asks: No, I haven't seen him/her/it anywhere today.

ref: We're honest men, and we come from the same family—we're not spies.”
text: Wo̱fẽ nū kome bii dšiwo̱; ano̱kwafoi dšiwo̱, dšee sralo̱i dši otšũdši.
translated: They have two types of children; the legitimate children, and the children born out of wedlock.

ref: But they explained, “Sir, we come from a family of twelve brothers. The youngest is still with our father in Canaan, and one of our brothers is dead.”
text: Ni ame̱kē̱: Otšũdši le̱ nyemimei nyoṅma ke̱ enyo̱ ni, nū kome bii dšiwo̱ ye̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱; ni na, gbekẽ le̱ ye̱ wo̱tše̱ ṅõ amrõ ne̱, ni mokome be̱.
translated: And he said: Your name will no longer be called Jacob, but Israel will be your name; and he named him Israel.

ref: Joseph replied: It's like I said. You're spies,
text: Ni Iosef kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Ene̱ mina mi mikē̱nye akē̱: Sralo̱i dšinye lē̱!
translated: But Joseph said to them: I am your brother: Do not be afraid!

ref: They did not know that Joseph could understand them, since he was speaking through an interpreter.
text: Ši ame̱ le̱ ame̱lee ake̱ Iosef enu noni ame̱ke̱ le̱; edšake̱ nādšielo̱ dšieo̱ ame̱nā.
translated: This person is the same Joseph they were talking about; that is why I am telling you about him.

ref: Joseph turned away from them and cried, but soon he turned back and spoke to them again. Then he had Simeon tied up and taken away while they watched. Joseph's Brothers Return to Canaan
text: Ni etšṍ ehe ke̱-dše ame̱ṅõ ni eyafó; ni ekũ ese̱ eba ame̱ṅõ ekoṅ ni ekeame̱ bawie; ni edšie Simeon ye̱ ame̱teṅ, ni efĩle ye̱ ame̱hĩe.
translated: Now there was a man in Jerusalem called Simeon, who was righteous and devout. He was waiting for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was upon him.

ref: But we told him, “We're honest men, not spies.
text: Ni wo̱kē̱le̱ ake̱: Ano̱kwafoi dšiwo̱, wo̱dšee sralo̱i;
translated: If you do this: Farmers plant, they will harvest.

ref: When the brothers started emptying their sacks of grain, they found their moneybags in them. They were frightened, and so was their father Jacob,
text: Ni eha mli ake̱, beni ame̱dšie ame̱kotokui le̱ amli nii le̱, na, mofẽmo šika ni afĩ le̱ ye̱ ekotoku le̱ mli. Ni beni ame̱na ame̱sika ni afĩmo̱fĩmo̱i le̱, ame̱ke̱ ame̱tše̱ fẽ še gbeyei.
translated: If you are inside, and you hear someone knocking at your door, do not rush to open the door immediately. If you do not know who is knocking, ask who it is before you open the door.

ref: who said, “You have already taken my sons Joseph and Simeon from me. And now you want to take away Benjamin! Everything is against me.”
text: Ni ame̱tše̱ Iakob kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyekpakpā mibii ye̱ mihe: Iosef be̱, ni Simeon be̱, ni nyebaṅõ Benyamin hũ ke̱-ya; ene̱mei ne̱ fẽ le̱ mino̱ kã!
translated: And Jacob their father said to them: You have bereaved me of my children: Joseph is gone, and Simeon is gone, and now you want to take Benjamin; all these things are against me!

ref: Judah replied, “The governor strictly warned us that we would not be allowed to see him unless we brought our youngest brother with us. If you let us take Benjamin along, we will go and buy grain. But we won't go without him!”
text: ši ke̱dši ohãṅ eya le̱, no le̱ wo̱woṅ yā; edšake̱ nū le̱ kē̱wo̱ ake̱: Nyenaṅ mihĩe, akē̱ dša nyenyemi le̱ fata nyehe.
translated: If you do this, you will see; but if you don't do it: Listen to me, I am telling you for your own good.

ref: Their father said: If Benjamin must go with you, take the governor a gift of some of the best things from our own country, such as perfume, honey, spices, pistachio nuts, and almonds.
text: Ni ame̱tše̱ Israel kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Ke̱dši nakai le̱, no le̱ bo̱ni nyefe ne̱: nyeṅõa šikpoṅ ne̱ no̱ yibii kpakpai le̱ eko nyewoa nyenibii amli, ni *nyeke̱-yakea nū le̱, gbonyowō tšofã fiō ke̱ wò fiō ke̱ tšofã-ke̱-ṅma ke̱ ohe̱ ni dšeo̱ ṅma, ke̱ amitšro̱bii ke̱ ṅme̱i; *Abe̱i
translated: When the people of Israel asked: What shall we eat, for we have no bread: God provided them with manna and quails, and with water from the rock, so that they ate and drank and were satisfied; He cared for them.

ref: he saw Benjamin and told the servant in charge of his house, “Take these men to my house. Slaughter an animal and cook it, so they can eat with me at noon.”
text: Ni beni Iosef na ake̱ Benyamin fata ame̱he le̱, ekē̱ ešĩa le̱ no̱ onukpa le̱ ake̱: Oke̱ ne̱kē̱ hĩĩ ne aya šĩa ni ogbe koloi oke̱-fe niyenii oto; ši hĩĩ ne̱ ke̱mi baye nii ſane ne̱.
translated: When Joseph saw his brother Benjamin with them, he said to the steward of his house, "Take these men to my house, slaughter an animal and prepare a meal; they are to eat with me at noon."

ref: together with enough money to buy more grain. We don't know who put the money in our sacks.”
text: ni šika kroko hũ wo̱ke̱-hĩe wo̱dẽ ke̱-ba niyenii heme̱ ne̱; wo̱lee moni ṅõ wo̱šika le̱ ewo wo̱kotokui le̱ amli.
translated: If you want to buy something and you don't have enough money; you can ask someone to lend you some money.

ref: After Joseph had asked how they were, he said, “What about your elderly father? Is he still alive?”
text: Ni ebiame̱ bo̱ni ame̱yo̱, ni ekē̱: Ani nyetše̱ nūmo le̱ *ni nyetšĩ etā le̱ ye̱ dšogbāṅ lo? Ani eye̱ dšeṅ lolo? *
translated: If you ask someone, say: Are you a priest or are you a chief? And what is your name?

ref: After washing his face and returning, he was able to control himself and said, “Serve the meal!”
text: Ni efo̱ ehĩe ni edše kpo, ni emĩa ehĩe, ni ekē̱: Nyeke̱ niyenii abamãmo̱a ši!
translated: If you wake up in the morning, in the afternoon, or at night: Always remember to pray!

ref: But they had not gone far from the city when Joseph told the servant, “Go after those men! When you catch them, say, ‘My master has been good to you. So why have you stolen his silver cup?
text: Beni ame̱dše kpo ye̱ mã le̱ṅ ni ame̱še̱ko šoṅṅ heko le̱, ke̱ke̱ ni Iosef kē̱ ešĩa le̱ no̱ onukpa le̱ ake̱: Te ši, ni otiu gbomei le̱, ni ké̱ oninaame̱ le̱, kē̱mo̱ame̱ ake̱: Menihewo̱ nyeke̱ efõṅ to ekpakpa nãdšiaṅ ne̱?
translated: When they had finished, they saw that the people who were sitting there, including Joseph who was an old man, asked: So, after all this, after you have heard everything, they asked: What does all this mean to us?

ref: Not only does he drink from his cup, but he also uses it to learn about the future. You have done a terrible thing.’ ”
text: Té ene̱ mli minuṅtšo̱ nuo̱ dā̃ ye̱, ni esā ekē̱-klaä? Nyefe efõṅ ake̱ nyefe nakai le̱!
translated: If you hear something like that, what will you do? Think about it very well!

ref: Joseph's servant started searching the sacks, beginning with the one that belonged to the oldest brother. When he came to Benjamin's sack, he found the cup.
text: Ni ebṍi taomo̱ ke̱-dše̱ onukpa le̱ no̱ ni eyagbe nā ye̱ gbekẽ le̱ no̱; ni ayana kpulu le̱ ye̱ Benyamin kotoku le̱ mli.
translated: When the day dawned, the east wind blew; and the children of Benjamin went out of the city.

ref: Judah went over to Joseph and said: Sir, you have as much power as the king himself, and I am only your slave. Please don't get angry if I speak.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iuda tši be̱ṅke̱le̱ ni ekē̱: Ofai ne̱, minuṅtšo̱! Hã ní otšũlo̱ awie wiemo̱ ko ye̱ minuṅtšo̱ toiiaṅ; ni kãhã omli mīwo otšũlo̱ la, ši otamo̱ Farao noṅṅ!
translated: Then Judah said to his brothers: Listen to me, please! We should not kill him; after all, he is our brother, our own flesh and blood. Instead, let’s sell him to the Ishmaelites, and not lay our hands on him, for he is our brother, our own flesh and blood.

ref: Then he cried so loudly that the Egyptians heard him and told about it in the king's palace.
text: Ni ewó egbē ni efó, ni Mizraimbii ke̱ Farao webii le̱ tete nu.
translated: And it happened in those days, in Egypt where Pharaoh was reigning at that time.

ref: Joseph was still crying as he kissed each of his other brothers. After this, they started talking with Joseph.
text: Ni esā eſo̱ſō̱ enyemimei le̱ fẽ nā, ni efó eſie ame̱no̱; ni no se̱ le̱ enyemimei le̱ ke̱le̱ wie.
translated: If you do good things for others today, you will also receive good things; if you do bad things, you will also receive bad things.

ref: When it was told in the palace that Joseph's brothers had come, the king and his officials were happy.
text: Ni anu sane ne̱ ye̱ Farao we le̱ ake̱: Iosef nyemimei eba! Ni Farao ke̱ etšũdši le̱ ná be mīše̱.
translated: This is what Pharaoh said to them: Go to Joseph! And Pharaoh told them to do whatever he tells you.

ref: So the king said to Joseph: Tell your brothers to load their donkeys and return to Canaan.
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Kē̱mo̱ onyemimei le̱ ake̱: Noni nyefe ne̱: nyetẽrea nyekoloi le̱ adšatšui, ni nyeyiaṅ ke̱-yaä Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱,
translated: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph: This is what I command you: Take wagons from Egypt, go and bring your father and your family, and come back to the land of Canaan.

ref: Also tell your brothers to take some wagons from Egypt for their wives and children to ride in. And be sure they bring their father.
text: Ni bo le̱ afā̃o ake̱: Noni nyefe ne̱: nyeṅõa ſīlii ke̱-dše̱a Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ ne̱ nyebāa nyebii ke̱ nyeṅãmei, ni nyeyaṅõa nyetše̱ ke̱-baä.
translated: This is what you must say: The Lord says: I brought you out of the land of Egypt where you were slaves, and you must not have any other gods besides me.

ref: Jacob's sons agreed to do what the king had said. And Joseph gave them wagons and food for their trip home, just as the king had ordered.
text: Ni Israel bihĩĩ le̱ fe nakai. Ni Iosef hāame̱ ſīlii; take̱ boni Farao fále̱ le̱, ni ehãame̱ gbe̱no̱ niyenii.
translated: When Israel arrived there, he called Joseph to him; then Pharaoh's house was told, and he (Pharaoh) was informed about it.

ref: Sixty-six members of Jacob's family went to Egypt with him, not counting his daughters-in-law.
text: Susumai fē ni ke̱ Iakob ba Mizraim ni dše̱ ehe̱ mli ne̱, sõro ebii le̱ aṅãmei le̱, *susumai le̱ fẽ dši nyoṅmai-ekpa ke̱ ekpa.
translated: The famine was so severe that Jacob had to go down to Egypt, where he and his ancestors had lived as strangers, because the famine was very severe in that land.

ref: I will let them live anywhere they choose in the land of Egypt, but I suggest that they settle in Goshen, the best part of our land. I would also like for your finest shepherds to watch after my own sheep and goats.”
text: Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ kã ohĩe; šikpoṅ le̱, beni hĩ fè fẽ ni dši Gošen šikpoṅ le̱, hã otše̱ ke̱ onyemimei le̱ ahĩ dše̱i; ni ke̱dši ole ame̱teṅ meikomei ṅí dši gbomei ni sa le̱, no le̱ ṅõame̱ ofe oṅukpai ye̱ mikoloi le̱ ano̱.
translated: Mizraim was in mourning for his son; he was mourning, but his sons and all those living in Goshen were mourning, and he did not eat with his family at the table; and he did not allow anyone to come near his house or to enter, because he did not want anyone to see the sorrow that was in his heart.

ref: “If you don't have any money,” Joseph answered, “give me your animals, and I'll let you have some grain.”
text: Ni Iosef ké̱: Ni nyešika etã ne̱, nyeṅõa nyekoloi nyehãa, ni mahãnye ye̱ nyekoloi le̱ anā.
translated: And Joseph said: I am going out, I will take my car, if you want you can sit in the car with me.

ref: “Will you give me your word?” Jacob asked. “Yes, I will,” Joseph promised. After this, Jacob bowed down and prayed at the head of his bed.
text: Ni eké̱: Kãmi kitã! Ni ekále̱ kitã. Ni Israel mã eyi ši ye̱ sātšo le̱ yiteṅ.
translated: And he said: Call him! And they called him. And Israel said to him, "Why have you done this?"

ref: Then Joseph made his sons move away from Jacob's knees, and Joseph bowed down in front of him with his face to the ground.
text: Ni Iosef dšieame̱ ke̱-dše̱ enakutšei ateṅ ke̱-ba, ni eho ekoto ši, eṅõ ehĩe ebu ši.
translated: When Joseph woke up from sleep, he did as the angel of the Lord commanded him, and took his wife.

ref: You will tie your donkey to a choice grapevine and wash your clothes in wine from those grapes.
text: Eefĩ etedšibi eṅmõ weintšo ke̱ etedšiyō bi hũ eṅmõ weintšonine; eke̱ wein aafo̱ etade he, ni ehebo le̱ eke̱ weinyibii amli lá aafo̱ he. Ioel
translated: If the elders eat, the children also eat; but if there is no food for the elders, then the children too will have nothing to eat. Joel

ref: Naphtali, you are a wild deer with lovely fawns.
text: Naftali le̱, ofrote ni he yo̱ oyá ni, ni ewieo̱ wiemo̱i ke̱ ṅõmo̱i. Kods.
translated: Naftali said, tell him that if he comes, he should bring the things. Thanks.

ref: Joseph, you are a fruitful vine growing near a stream and climbing a wall.
text: Iosef le̱, tšo ni woo̱ yibii ni, nu ni baä he tšo ni woo̱ yibii ni, ni enidši le̱ kwō̱lo̱ö̱ gbogbo. Hos.
translated: Joseph said, "Do not be afraid, for am I in the place of God?" Hos.

ref: Both the cave and the land that goes with it were bought from the Hittites.
text: Abō̱ le̱ ke̱ te̱kplo ni yo̱ mli le̱ ahe ye̱ Hetbii le̱ adeṅ.
translated: The goat is in the room and the sheep is outside.

ref: When the time of mourning was over, Joseph said to the Egyptian leaders, “If you consider me your friend, please speak to the king for me.
text: Ni beni efómo̱ gbĩĩ le̱ ebo le̱, Iosef ke̱ Farao we le̱ bii le̱ wie ni ekē̱: Agbe̱ne̱ ke̱dši mihĩe eba nyam ye̱ nyehĩe le̱, mīkpanye fai, nyewoa Farao toi no̱ ake.
translated: When the famine had spread over all the land, Joseph opened all the storehouses and sold grain to the Egyptians, for the famine was severe throughout Egypt.

ref: You tried to harm me, but God made it turn out for the best, so that he could save all these people, as he is now doing.
text: Nye le̱, nyesusumi efõṅ; ši *Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, esusule̱ ekpakpa, beni afe ni ahere mei babaõ yiwala take̱ bo̱ni eba mli ùmene ne.
translated: As for me, I have finished my work; but *God has not finished his great work, and so every day he continues to do good things for us.

ref: If we don't outsmart them, their families will keep growing larger. And if our country goes to war, they could easily fight on the side of our enemies and escape from Egypt.
text: Nyebaä, nyehãa woke̱ame̱ ayea ṅā mli, koni ame̱yi akafá ní ebaba mli ake, ké̱ ta ko bate ši le̱, ni ame̱ hũ ame̱yafata wo̱henye̱lo̱i le̱ ahe ní ame̱ke̱wo̱ ṅwu ní ame̱mīfã ke̱-ṅdše šikpoṅ le̱ no̱!
translated: Listen, I have spoken my mind, but if someone thinks what I have said is not good, let them also say theirs, so that we can all learn from each other and move forward together!

ref: But even though the Israelites were mistreated, their families grew larger, and they took over more land. Because of this, the Egyptians feared them worse than before
text: Ši babaō ní ame̱waäame̱ yi le̱, babaō ní ame̱yi fáa ní ame̱ſereo̱ dši no, ni ame̱tšui suo̱ Israelbii le̱ ano̱.
translated: The fathers ate sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge, so it will be with the people of Israel.

ref: and made them work so hard
text: Ni Mizraimbii le̱ hĩe Israelbii le̱ nyṍdšiahĩemo̱ še̱ī,
translated: When the Egyptians were oppressing the Israelites,

ref: The king called them in again and asked, “Why are you letting those baby boys live?”
text: Ni Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ tšé̱ yeife̱yei le̱ ni ekē̱ame̱ ake: Mẽhewo̱ nyefe nakai ni nyehere gbekẽbiihĩĩ le̱ ayiwala le̱?
translated: When the Egyptians see you, they will say, "This is his wife." Then they will kill me but let you live.

ref: Some shepherds tried to chase them away, but Moses came to their rescue and watered their animals.
text: Ši tōkwe̱lo̱i le̱ ba baſieame̱; ni Mose te ši, ni efã amehe, ni *ehã ametōi le̱ anu. *
translated: He led them out of the city; not Moses did so, not any person, but God Himself did it.

ref: After the death of the king of Egypt, the Israelites still complained because they were forced to be slaves. They cried out for help,
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ nakai gbĩĩ pī le̱ amli le̱ Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ bagbo, ni Israelbii le̱ dõmo̱ ntšo̱i ye̱ nyṍṅyeli le̱ hewo, ni ame̱fó ame̱tšé̱ Nyoṅmo̱; ni ame-nyṍṅyeli le̱ mli fómo̱-ke̱-tšémo̱ le̱ *yase̱ Nyoṅmo̱ ṅõ. *
translated: When they were in Egypt and were suffering, and the Israelites cried out because of their oppression, God heard them; and God saw their suffering and remembered His covenant.

ref: and God heard their loud cries. He did not forget the promise he had made to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob,
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ nu ame̱ṅke̱mo̱yeli blo̱mo̱ le̱, ni Nyoṅmo̱ kai *ekpãṅmo̱ ní eke̱ Abraham ke̱ Isak ke̱ Iakob kpãṅ le̱; *
translated: But God is not the God of the dead, but of the living; for to him all are alive.

ref: Call together the leaders of Israel and tell them that the God who was worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob has appeared to you. Tell them I have seen how terribly they are being treated in Egypt,
text: Ya ní oyabua Israel onukpai le̱ anā, ni okē̱ame̱ ake̱: Iehowa, nyetše̱mei a-Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, ní dši Abraham ke̱ Isak ke̱ Iakob Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, edšie ehe kpo etšõmi, ni ekē̱: Sramo̱ mibasranye, ni mina noni afeo̱nye ye̱ Mizraim le̱;
translated: And when the people of Israel cried out to the Lord, the Lord said: I am the Lord your God, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. I have heard your cry, and I say: I have delivered you, and brought you out of Egypt;

ref: The Lord sent Aaron to meet Moses in the desert. So Aaron met Moses at Mount Sinai and greeted him with a kiss.
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱ Aaron ake̱: Ya ní oke̱ Mose ayakpe ye̱ ṅã le̱ no̱. Ni ete ní eke̱le̱ yakpe ye̱ Nyoṅmo̱ goṅ le̱ he, ni eſo̱ enā.
translated: And the Lord said to Aaron: Go and meet Moses in the wilderness. So he went and met him at the mountain of God, and kissed him.

ref: The king said, “Moses and Aaron, why are you keeping these people from working? Look how many you are keeping from doing their work. Now everyone get back to work!”
text: Ni Farao sā kē̱: Na, agbe̱ne̱ maṅ le̱ *yi efa momo ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni nyehã ame̱dšo̱ö̱ amehe noṅṅ ne̱! *
translated: Then Pharaoh said: Well, let no one add or take away from the work, and let everyone do their own share!

ref: That same day the king gave orders to his Egyptian slave bosses and to the Israelite men directly in charge of the Israelite slaves. He told them:
text: Ni nakai gbĩ le̱ no̱ noṅṅ le̱ Farao wo maṅ le̱ kplalo̱i ke̱ ame̱no̱kwe̱lo̱i le̱ akitã ake̱:
translated: And it happened in the morning when Pharaoh went to the river, there were women and maidens washing clothes by the river bank:

ref: Don't give the slaves any more straw to put in their bricks. Force them to find their own straw wherever they can,
text: Nyekahãa maṅ le̱ dſe̱i ame̱ṅõ-fe ntayāi le̱ doṅṅ tamo̱ tšutšu le̱; ame̱dientše̱ ameya ní ame̱yabuabua dſe̱i anā ame̱hã amehe;
translated: Let us love one another just as God has loved us; if we love each other, God lives in us and his love is made complete in us;

ref: Make them work so hard that they won't have time to listen to these lies.
text: Ahã nitšumo̱ le̱ anyẽ gbomei le̱, koni ato̱ame̱ ní ame̱kabo yakawiemo̱i atoi!
translated: If you do not help your friend, then when you are in trouble, no one will help you!

ref: The bosses beat the men in charge of the slaves and said, “Why didn't you force the slaves to make as many bricks yesterday and today as they did before?”
text: Ni ayi Israelbii le̱ ano̱kwe̱lo̱i ní Farao kplalo̱i le̱ ṅõ-to ame̱no̱ le̱ ake̱: Mẽhewo̱ ni nyè ke̱ ṅme̱ne̱ fẽ nyegbee nyentayāi ní atonye le̱ anā tamo̱ sá le̱?
translated: Then the Israelites said to them, "May the Lord look upon you and judge you! You have made us a stench to Pharaoh and his officials and have put a sword in their hand to kill us."

ref: Finally, the men in charge of the slaves went to the king and said, “Why are you treating us like this?
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Israelbii le̱ ano̱kwe̱lo̱i le̱ ba ni ame̱bablo̱ ame̱wo Farao ame̱kē̱: Mẽhewo̱ oke̱ otšũdši yeo̱ ne̱ke̱?
translated: The Israelites said to them, "Why have you done this to us by bringing us out of Egypt?"

ref: Then the men said, “We hope the Lord will punish both of you for making the king and his officials hate us. Now they even have an excuse to kill us.” The Lord's Promise to Moses
text: ni ame̱kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Iehowa aakwe̱ ní ekodšo, edšake̱ nyehã wohe *edše fũ ewo Farao ke̱ etšũdši le̱, ni nyeṅõ klante nyewo ame̱dẽ ní ame̱ke̱-gbewo̱. *
translated: This is what it means: Jehovah said to me, “Go to Pharaoh, for I have made his heart and the hearts of his officials stubborn, so that I may show these signs of mine among them.”

ref: But the Lord sent Aaron and Moses with a message for the Israelites and for the king. He also ordered Aaron and Moses to free the people from Egypt. Family Record of Aaron and Moses
text: Ni Iehowa ke̱ Mose ke̱ Aaron wie, ni efã́ame̱ ake̱ ameya Israelbii le̱ ke̱ Mizraim maṅtše̱ Farao le̱ ṅõ, ní ame̱ya-dšie Israelbii le̱ ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ ne̱.
translated: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, and he commanded them to bring the Israelites out of Egypt, out of Pharaoh’s land, so that the Israelites would leave Egypt in groups.

ref: Merari's sons were Mahli and Mushi. All of the above were from the Levi tribe.
text: Ni Merari bii ne̱: Maheli ke̱ Muši. Mene̱mei dši Levi wekui le̱ ye̱ ame̱fo̱mo̱i le̱ anā.
translated: Merari had two sons: Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of the Levites, listed according to their families.

ref: Korah's sons were Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph.
text: Ni Kora bii ne̱: Asir ke̱ Elkana ke̱ Abiasaf. Mene̱mei dši Korabii wekui le̱.
translated: The sons of Korah are: Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph. These are the families of the Korahites.

ref: The Lord had commanded Aaron and Moses to lead every family and tribe of Israel out of Egypt,
text: Mene̱mei dši Aaron ke̱ Mose ni Iehowa kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyeyadšiea Israelbii le̱ ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ye̱ ame̱tai le̱ anā le̱.
translated: The Lord spoke to Aaron and Moses, saying: Bring the people of Israel out of the land of Egypt according to their divisions.

ref: he said, “I am the Lord. Tell the king of Egypt everything I say to you.”
text: ake̱ Iehowa kē̱ Mose ake̱: Midši Iehowa le̱; kē̱mo̱ Farao, Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱, nii fẽ ní mike̱o wieo̱ le̱.
translated: Then the Lord said to Moses: Go to the Lord; for I have hardened Pharaoh, the king of Egypt's heart, so that I may show my signs among them.

ref: The king behaved just as the Lord had said and stubbornly refused to listen. The Nile River Turns into Blood
text: Ni awadše Farao tšui, ni ebooame̱ toi, take̱ bo̱ni Iehowa *ewie le̱. *
translated: When Pharaoh stubbornly refused, the Lord struck him, so that he would know that the Lord is God.

ref: Tomorrow morning take the stick that turned into a snake, then wait beside the Nile River for the king.
text: Ya Farao ṅõ lebi; na, ēdše kpo eya fā le̱ nā, ni oyadamo̱ fā le̱ nā omẽle̱, ni tšo le̱ ní tšṍ onũfũ le̱ ko̱ ohĩe odẽ,
translated: But Pharaoh refused; so, he hardened his heart, and did not let the people go, just as the Lord had said.

ref: “The Lord is going to do something to show you that he really is the Lord. I will strike the Nile with this stick, and the water will turn into blood.
text: Boni Iehowa kē̱ ne̱: Ene̱ oke̱-ale ake̱ midši Iehowa le̱: na, mike̱ tšo ní mihĩe ye̱ midẽ ne̱ batſa fā le̱ mli nu le̱, ni *eetšṍ lá. *
translated: The Lord says this: This is what the Lord Almighty says: See, I am going to remove the disaster from this city, and it will not happen.

ref: the fish died, and the water smelled so bad that none of the Egyptians could drink it. Blood was everywhere in Egypt.
text: Ni lōi ní yo̱ fā le̱ mli le̱ gboi, ni fā le̱ ša, ni Mizraimbii le̱ nyẽẽ fā le̱ mli nu le̱ anu; ni lá so̱ṅṅ yo̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ fẽ.
translated: When you were living in the city, you suffered, and in Egypt you also suffered in the city because of hunger; and that is why you left Egypt to come here.
